data.body:
Design brainstorm (/joe-brainstorm) refining epic rule-swamp-pupil (ab74-b205-72af-4ce1) — P1.2 rebar export/import. Grounded in codebase investigation (subagents) + prior art (git-bug, git-issue).

DECISIONS (all confirmed by Joe):
1. Purpose: lossy NDJSON interop projection. Primary = reporting/data-mining; secondary = clean rebar-to-rebar migration. Backup explicitly OUT of scope (git bundle of the tickets branch is already lossless and preserves the audit trail).
2. Format: NDJSON, one full ticket object per line — serves reporting (DuckDB/jq/pandas/BigQuery) AND migration (each line re-imports as a full ticket) from one artifact. Per-line schema_version; run metadata (exported_at, source_env, counts) to stderr.
3. Jira omission: provider-neutral --strip-external (alias --no-jira) strips jira_comment_id, bridge_alerts, SYNC, and any future provider id. This is the only seam the GitHub follow-on inherits (no reconciler coupling).
4. Migration fidelity: provenance fields (source_id/source_created_at/source_author/source_env); fresh ids + fresh HLC timestamps (never inject foreign timestamps — HLC monotonicity/clock-domain hazard). STATUS events emitted to reproduce non-open states (CREATE only yields `open` — gap fixed).
5. Re-import: idempotent skip by source_id (streaming target scan builds source_id->local_id). Never updates existing tickets (that is sync = reconciler).
6. Surface: CLI + library only. MCP export_tickets/import_tickets DROPPED — NDJSON is not an MCP return shape, whole-store dumps flood agent context, and the real flows are CLI->file.
7. Performance: export streams via reduce_ticket (reduce_all_tickets materializes all states — memory risk at scale). Import defers push (REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off) + single final push. Batch-commit primitive DEFERRED (residual limit: one git commit + one lock cycle per event; ~3.3 min lock overhead for ~2000 events).

CRITICAL EVALUATION — GitHub sync vs import (Joe's explicit question): GitHub Issues sync (parity with Jira) must ride the EXISTING reconciler (binding store + bidirectional differs + applier), NOT this import path. Import is stateless/additive/lossy; sync needs a persistent {provider,external_key} binding + field-level diff + conflict resolution + convergent idempotency. The reconciler is hardcoded to Jira in 6+ places (jira_key, jira- id prefix, ADF codec, type/status/priority maps, reconciler-echo marker, rebar-id: labels); de-Jira-ifying it is the follow-on epic's spine. Export/import neither helps nor blocks that work, except by leaving the provider-neutral --strip-external association seam.

TICKETS:
- rule-swamp-pupil (ab74-b205-72af-4ce1): description rewritten to the refined plan; clarity 7/pass, AC pass.
- be74-7832-03a8-48ac: NEW follow-on epic "GitHub Issues sync — provider parity with the Jira reconciler", linked discovered_from the epic.
- 39c5-d81a-aad6-4e21: NEW follow-up task "Batch-commit write primitive for bulk operations", linked discovered_from the epic.

STATUS: design complete, recorded. No code written yet — implementation not started.
